title: Operator ordering as an emergent geometric background in Dirac systems with spatially varying mass
abstract: We investigate the spectral consequences of the uniquely determined Hermitian ordering of the Dirac Hamiltonian with spatially varying mass. In contrast to the nonrelativistic case, where continuous families of admissible prescriptions exist, the relativistic Dirac operator admits a single consistent ordering compatible with probability-current conservation. This requirement generates an additional logarithmic-gradient term proportional to the spatial variation of the mass profile. We show that this contribution modifies the effective kinetic operator and induces a universal deformation of the spectral quantization condition. In compact geometry, an explicit analytic computation reveals a mode-dependent second-order spectral shift that becomes strongly enhanced near the mass-inversion threshold. These results demonstrate that the consistent relativistic ordering of the Dirac operator leads to observable modifications of discrete spectra in spatially inhomogeneous scalar backgrounds.
summary: This paper demonstrates that the requirement for probability-current conservation in relativistic systems uniquely fixes the operator ordering for spatially varying mass. This fixed ordering acts as an emergent geometric background that causes measurable shifts in the energy spectra of Dirac particles.
main_problem: The paper addresses the ambiguity of operator ordering in the Dirac Hamiltonian when mass varies spatially, specifically investigating if the unique ordering required for probability-current conservation leads to observable spectral effects.
main_result: The study finds that the required Hermitian ordering introduces an effective geometric connection that induces a universal, second-order spectral shift, which becomes strongly enhanced near the mass-inversion threshold.
method: The authors use analytical derivation based on Hermiticity and current conservation requirements, combined with second-order perturbation theory and explicit computation in a 1D ring geometry.
model_or_system: A single-particle Dirac field coupled to a spatially varying scalar mass profile m(x), with specific analysis applied to a 1D ring with angular mass modulation.
key_observables: Probability current, spectral quantization condition, energy level shifts, and the effective geometric connection (holonomy).
important_parameters: Logarithmic gradient of the mass profile, mass-inversion threshold, modulation amplitude (epsilon), and the geometric scale (grad m / m).
main_assumptions: The mass profile is assumed to be smooth, single-valued, and periodic; the derivation focuses on the single-particle Hamiltonian.
figures_summary: Figure 1(a) shows the evolution of energy levels (En) as a function of the mass modulation amplitude (delta m/m0).
paper_structure: The paper identifies the problem of operator ordering ambiguity, derives the unique Hermitian Hamiltonian required for current conservation, compares the relativistic case to the non-relativistic case, and then provides analytical results for spectral shifts in a ring geometry.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

(a) Energy levels En as a function of the modulation amplitude δm/m0 for quantum numbers n = −2, . . . , 2.
